tweets.text: Btw, for Ghostling (libghostty demo), I didn't write a single line of... anything. Agents wrote 100% of everything you see incl. Nix flakes, CI jobs, etc. I reviewed every line of code manually and constantly nudged the agents in the right direction. I used a mix of Opus+Codex. https://t.co/tRkiHoyTPT

For pure theoretical physics can’t see how this wouldn’t be 2027

Window of final human-only cognitive contributions is coming to a close

Forever

surprising fact: for any random variable x,

| mean(x) - median(x) |  ≤  std(x)

It’s not hard to show

|μ - m| = |E(X - m)| ≤ E|X - m|
≤ E|X - μ| = E√(X - μ)²
≤ √ E(X - μ)²
= σ 

originally appeared in a paper by Hotelling & Solomons Annals of Mathematical Statistics 1932 https://t.co/eUOhmRCyaV

I started Computer with 2 principles:
- only implement the most generic thing
- anything it can’t do is a bug

Amazing as a technologist to be here.

Today is multi-agentic orchestration day at Gauntlet AI.

Challengers are presenting the systems they built to let AI solve increasingly complex problems autonomously. https://t.co/6qsuKqCNf0

I don’t think AIs should be auto-adding themselves as credited on projects on Github or elsewhere.

It primarily serves as a marketing tool to promote the product, but undermines the much more critical aspect that humans should be able to choose their relationship with AI work.

What we have been saying for several years now: AI changes everything. 

One of the best mathematicians alive Terence Tao agrees: 

'Yeah, so we live in a time of change. It is, as I said, we live in a particularly unpredictable era. And I think things that we've taken for

Evidence that AI models can, indeed, learn "taste" in this paper where a small model, trained on citations, is able to predict which papers will be hits

Citations, upvotes & shares are signals that can teach AI judgment about quality, not just execution. https://t.co/2KWBQIMqt0 https://t.co/nI26UhdI9c

Do people like this? We don't do this for codex because it exists to help you and it's important that you remain the owner and accountable for your work without AI taking credit. At the same time it does mean that you can't trace how popular codex is among repos.

libghostty is FAST! Here's a look at vtebench with Ghostling. Reminder: Ghostling is single threaded + blocking render + blocking IO. The point is, you get comparable speed to the fastest dedicated terminals in an embeddable lib! Had to pull out iTerm2 because its so slow. https://t.co/ij8qfDCMA7

The new Stitch design AI from Google Labs is fanstastic, it creates beautiful designs that I like & you can also edit them. You can then send screens to AI Studio to build great websites or apps. Here’s an example I made very quickly.  Link to the web site below in the thread. https://t.co/s5eAf5Q6vI
Gave For You a shot, back to Following as default timeline. Better experience following & interacting with real people moving AI.
gpt-5.4 for frontend:

Data-driven optimization works best when we can leverage structure. In offline RL, MDP structure enables temporal compositionality ("stitching"), while for protein design, analogous graph structure can make design of new proteins more efficient!
I tested BioReason-pro. It's a very cool reasoning BioAI model for protein function, and it also has a very nice UI. Anyone interested should give it a try.

The biggest reason OpenClaw took off is because you could actually do stuff with it.

Claude is supposedly building out the tools to allow you to do stuff, but everything is built with kid gloves in mind. https://t.co/euqYthTAP0

Public markets are right to devalue vertical SAAS revenue given the plummeting cost of recreation

An nnevitable consequence of superintelligence is that vertical software becomes hyper competitive.

Horizontal software however accumulates value as adding features becomes easier

Google is the default search engine on Comet iOS (unlike on Comet desktop): Most mobile browser searches are around navigating to restaurant or local shops, checking scores, shopping, hotels. Google does a much better job here than anyone else in the world, including Perplexity.
